title: Immigration (general)
framing: Immigration is staged as open versus closed, but the country argues past itself because two goods are on the table — the dignity of the person who moves for a better life, and the security of the citizen who receives them. Both are real. The fight scrambles party because the security good is mostly spoken in a racial vehicle that discredits it, and the welcome good keeps forgetting the cause that produced the refugee and the consequence that follows them in.
prompt: Two goods in tension — the dignity of the one who moves and the security of the one who receives.

fluid.text: We made promises to the persecuted — asylum is a covenant, honor it.

coalition.text: No human is illegal — legalize them, protect them.

denominated.text: The border itself is violence — any enforcement is cruelty.

fluid.text: A country has to decide who enters — that's self-government, not hatred.

coalition.text: We've lost control of the border — enforce it, build the wall.

denominated.text: This is an invasion — a deliberate replacement of a people.

fluid.text: Immigrants do the work and build the businesses — the economy needs them.

coalition.text: Let people move — strivers should get to chase a better life.

denominated.text: Borders are just protectionism with a flag — abolish them.

fluid.text: A real border lets us keep government small — secure the line, leave us alone.

coalition.text: We can't be the world's welfare magnet — a country isn't a charity.

denominated.text: They come to take — a drain on the people who actually build.

LF.text: People seeking a better life deserve dignity — welcome them well.

LC.text: No human is illegal — protect them.

LD.text: The border itself is violence; any enforcement is cruelty.

RF.text: A country has to be able to secure its border and govern who enters.

RD.text: It's an invasion — they're replacing us.
